'''Dominique Peyroux''' (born 21 January 1989)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/news-display/Gold-Coasts-Peyroux-signs-for-Vodafone-Warriors/65569|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130223041056/http://www.warriors.co.nz/news-display/Gold-Coasts-Peyroux-signs-for-Vodafone-Warriors/65569|url-status=dead|archive-date=23 February 2013|title=Gold Coast's Peyroux signs for Vodafone Warriors|first=Richard|last=Becht|work=warriors.co.nz|date=1 November 2012|access-date=16 November 2012}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url= http://www.nrl.com/telstrapremiership/playerstats/playerprofile/tabid/10898/clubid/16/playerid/1214/seasonid/10/default.aspx|title=Player Profile|work=nrl.com|access-date=16 November 2012}}</ref> is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward or {{rlp|ce}} for [[Racing Club Albi XIII|Albi]] in the [[Super XIII]]. He has played for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] and the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]]. Peyroux represented the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] at the [[2009 Pacific Cup]], [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]] and the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]], and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]].

==Early years==
Peyroux was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, to a father of [[Cook Islands|Cook Island]] and [[French people|French]] descent and a mother of [[Samoans|Samoan]] and [[Solomon Islands|Solomon Island]] descent.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl|title=NRL | NRL and Rugby League News | NRL and Rugby League News | Gold Coast Bulletin}}</ref><ref name=journey>{{cite web|last=Burgess|first=Michael|date=18 November 2012|title=Peyroux's long journey home|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10848135|work=New Zealand Herald|access-date=4 May 2019}}</ref>

He played junior rugby league for the [[Otahuhu Leopards]], [[Mangere East Hawks]], and Papatoetoe Panthers.<ref>[http://www.warriors.co.nz/news-display/Gold%20Coasts%20Peyroux%20signs%20for%20Vodafone%20Warriors/65569 Gold Coast's Peyroux signs for Vodafone Warriors] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121103210821/http://www.warriors.co.nz/news-display/Gold%20Coasts%20Peyroux%20signs%20for%20Vodafone%20Warriors/65569 |date=3 November 2012 }} ''warriors.co.nz'', 1 November 2012</ref><ref name=journey /> He attended and played for the Sarah Redfern High School after moving to Australia with his family at age 15 to pursue his rugby league career. Peyroux signed with the [[Sydney Roosters]], and then moved to Mattraville Sports High School in his final year of schooling, winning the [[ARL Schoolboy Cup|AAC National Champions Trophy]] with the school in 2007.<ref name="Matraville Win 1st Arrive Alive Cup">{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/arrive_alive_cup.php|title=Matraville Win 1st Arrive Alive Cup|publisher=ourfootyteam.com|date=23 September 2007|access-date=24 October 2009}}</ref>

==Playing career==
[[File:Dominique Peyroux.jpg|thumb|Peyroux in 2008]]
Peyroux signed professionally for the Sydney Roosters in 2007 but never featured for their first grade side, instead playing in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] in the Under 20 set-up.<ref name="Dominique Peyroux Profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/p/peyroux_dominique/index.php|title= Dominique Peyroux Profile|publisher=rleague.com|date=3 July 2009|access-date=3 July 2009}}</ref> He then featured for Sydney's feeder side, the [[Newtown Jets]], in the [[New South Wales Cup]] for the 2008 season,<ref name="Dominique Peyroux Profile Image @ Newtown Jets 2008 Squad Webpage">{{cite web|url=http://www.newtownjets.com/2008-gallery/2008-player-gallery.php|title=Dominique Peyroux Profile Image at Newtown Jets 2008 Squad Webpage|publisher=newtownjets.com|date=4 September 2008|access-date=4 September 2009|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090914061451/http://www.newtownjets.com/2008-gallery/2008-player-gallery.php|archive-date=14 September 2009|df=dmy}}</ref> before joining the [[Gold Coast Titans]].<ref name="Dominique Peyroux Profile"/> In 2010, he was dual registered with [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]], a feeder club to the Titans.

He made his debut for the Titans in 2011 and played sixteen games for the club as they finished last on the table and claimed the wooden spoon.<ref name=rlp>[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/dominique-peyroux/summary.html Dominique Peyroux] ''rugbyleagueproject.org''</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/parramatta-eels-defeat-the-gold-coast-titans-to-avoid-wooden-spoon-for-2011/news-story/fc87216a6a23a4b8d0eefd6174b60c47/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat the Gold Coast Titans to avoid wooden spoon for 2011|website=Daily Telegraph}}</ref>

Peyroux joined the [[New Zealand Warriors]] for the 2013 season and made his debut for the club on 3 June 2013.<ref>[http://www.nrl.com/titans-off-load-two-more-to-nrl-rivals/tabid/10874/newsid/70325/default.aspx Titans off-load two more to NRL rivals] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121103051225/http://www.nrl.com/titans-off-load-two-more-to-nrl-rivals/tabid/10874/newsid/70325/default.aspx |date=3 November 2012 }} ''nrl.com'', 1 November 2012</ref>

He signed for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], and started on a two-year contract at the beginning of 2016 season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/jul/23/st-helens-dominique-peyroux-new-zealand-warriors|title=St Helens sign Dominique Peyroux from New Zealand Warriors|newspaper=The Guardian |agency=Press Association|date=23 July 2015|via=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|work=Daily Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Salford Red Devils]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|work=The Daily Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

On 17 Nov 2020 it was announced that Peyroux would be joining his teammate [[Joseph Paulo]] at [[Toulouse Olympique]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/18878225.dominique-peyroux-swaps-st-helens-toulouse-olympique/|title=Dominique Peyroux swaps St Helens for Toulouse Olympique|website=St Helens Star|date=17 November 2020 |access-date=18 Nov 2020}}</ref>

He played in St Helens 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport |date=25 November 2020 }}</ref>

=== Toulouse Olympique ===
On 17 November 2020, Toulouse announced the signing of Peyroux from St Helens on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ladepeche.fr/2020/11/17/rugby-a-xiii-le-toulouse-olympique-trouve-encore-son-bonheur-a-saint-helens-9205660.php|title=Rugby à XIII : Le Toulouse Olympique trouve encore son bonheur à Saint-Helens}}</ref>
On 10 October 2021, Peyroux played for Toulouse in their victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[Million Pound Game]] which saw the club promoted to the [[Super League]] for the first time in their history. On 15 October 2023, Peyroux played in Toulouse Olympique's upset loss against the London Broncos in the RFL Championship Grand Final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12430948/million-pound-game-toulouse-promoted-to-super-league-after-beating-featherstone#:~:text=Toulouse%20beat%20part%2Dtimers%20Featherstone,preferred%20French%20team%20in%202003/|title=Toulouse promoted to Super League after beating Featherstone|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport |date=15 October 2023 }}</ref>

On 20 October 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Toulouse Olympique]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Oct 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Former St Helens and Hull KR stars among trio of exits from Toulouse|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-st-helens-and-hull-kr-stars-among-trio-of-exits-from-toulouse}}</ref>

===Albi===
On 8 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Racing Club Albi XIII|Albi]] in the [[Super XIII]] for the remainder of the 2025/26 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=8 Nov 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former St Helens star lands new club following Super League release|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/dom-peyroux-joins-albi-following-toulouse-release}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Peyroux was selected for the Cook Islands for their [[2009 Pacific Cup]] competition in October 2009 and featured against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], in a 22–20 win to qualify for the group stages,<ref name="Cook Islands progress to Pacific Cup">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57129/cook-islands-progress-to-pacific-cup/default.aspx|title=Cook Islands progress to Pacific Cup|publisher=nrl.com|date=4 October 2009|access-date=18 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091019115213/http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57129/cook-islands-progress-to-pacific-cup/default.aspx|archive-date=19 October 2009|url-status=dead}}</ref> and against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in another narrow win; 24–22 in which Peyroux, playing on the {{rlp|wg}}, scored twice, the second of which being the match-winning try in the final minute.<ref name="Cook Islands continue run of upsets">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57129/cook-islands-progress-to-pacific-cup/default.aspx|title=Cook Islands continue run of upsets|publisher=news.theage.com.au|date=24 October 2009|access-date=24 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091019115213/http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57129/cook-islands-progress-to-pacific-cup/default.aspx|archive-date=19 October 2009|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Giant killers strike again - Cook Islands defeat Fiji">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57172/giant-killers-strike-again---cook-islands-defeat-fiji/default.aspx|title=Giant killers strike again - Cook Islands defeat Fiji|publisher=nrl.com|date=24 October 2009|access-date=25 October 2009|archive-date=29 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091029050203/http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57172/giant-killers-strike-again---cook-islands-defeat-fiji/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="Cook Islands snatch 24-22 win over Fiji">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57171/cook-islands-snatch-24-22-win-over-fiji/default.aspx|title=Cook Islands snatch 24-22 win over Fiji|publisher=nrl.com|date=24 October 2009|access-date=25 October 2009|archive-date=27 October 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091027212526/http://www.nrl.com/newsviews/latestnews/newsarticle/tabid/10874/newsid/57171/cook-islands-snatch-24-22-win-over-fiji/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref> This win guided them to the final against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] on 1 November 2009. They lost this final by 42–14, a game in which Peyroux scored his third try in as many games in the tournament.<ref name="PNG win place in Four Nations">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/png-win-place-in-four-nations/story-e6frexnr-1225793305420|title=PNG win place in Four Nations|publisher=dailytelegraph.com.au|date=1 November 2009|access-date=5 November 2009}}</ref>

In October and November 2013, Peyroux played for 'the Kukis' in their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign. He scored a try in 2 of their 3 games in the tournament.

In May 2014, Peyroux played for another Pacific Island Nation. He represented Samoa in the 2014 Pacific Rugby League International.

On 7 October 2014, Peyroux was selected in the Samoan 24-man squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] series. He was only used once in the campaign.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2014/10/07/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|date=7 October 2014|website=National Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/ |title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad | NRL | Zero Tackle |access-date=1 March 2016 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203031215/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/ |archive-date=3 February 2016 |df=dmy-all}}</ref>

On 2 May 2015, Peyroux scored the winning try after a line-break in their [[Polynesian Cup]] match against Tonga.

==Personal life==
Whilst playing for the Roosters in 2008, Peyroux featured in the ''Naked for a Cause'' initiative ''Gods of Football'' in which [[Australian Football League|AFL]] and [[Australian Rugby League|ARL]] players compiled a nude calendar to raise money for [[breast cancer]] charity the [[McGrath Foundation]].<ref name="Gods of Football Website">{{cite web|url=http://godsoffootball.com|title=Gods of Football Website|publisher=godsoffootball.com|date=25 October 2009|access-date=25 October 2009}}</ref><ref name="Dominique Peyroux Gods of Football Image">{{cite web|url=http://www.cosmopolitan.com.au/images/cosmo/gallery/Love%20and%20Sex/Man%20of%20the%20moment/Gods%20of%20Football/dominique-peyroux.thumb.jpg|title=Dominique Peyroux Gods of Football Image|publisher=cosmopolitan.com.au|date=25 October 2009|access-date=7 November 2009}}</ref>
